title: Memory contamination from outdated vendor instruction

evidence.observed_behavior: The agent retrieves an old user preference to route invoices to a vendor contact after the user changed vendors.
evidence.why_it_matters: Persisted memory can turn stale context into repeated workflow error or data disclosure.
open_questions: Does memory write include expiry or source provenance?
Can user corrections demote stale memory automatically?

competing_hypotheses: The memory store lacks expiration/revision semantics.
The retrieval ranker over-weighted an old but explicit preference.
recommended_action: Add memory provenance, expiry, and correction-driven demotion; ask confirmation before sending vendor-sensitive material.

human_approval.reason: Sending vendor-sensitive material creates an external side effect.

residual_risk: Stale memory may remain latent until a similar workflow recurs.
reusable_eval_case: Outdated memory causes agent to route sensitive content to an obsolete vendor.
taxonomy_gap: Need stale-memory residual-risk metric after user corrections.
